sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a history of a trampoline injury four days ago, resulting in a pop and significant swelling in his right knee. He describes the pain as sharp and constant, particularly with weight-bearing. He has taken ibuprofen and applied ice, which provided some relief but the pain persists. He admits to occasionally forgetting to check his blood sugar levels and enjoys ice cream regularly. He reports that his diet has been pretty good lately. He states that his primary care physician is treating his diabetes.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The right knee exhibits significant swelling and limited range of motion. Palpation reveals tenderness to the medial aspect of the knee. The McBurney's test is negative. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally. Heart sounds are normal with no murmurs or gallops. Vital signs are within normal limits.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's presentation is consistent with a possible medial collateral ligament (MCL) tear, given the reported pop and significant swelling. The pain and limited range of motion suggest an injury to the knee joint. The patient's diabetes is being managed by his primary care physician.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe a straight leg brace for immobilization. Prescribe mobic for pain relief and edema reduction. Order an MRI of the right knee to evaluate for ligamentous injury and other structural damage. Recheck hemoglobin A1c in four weeks. Refill metformin 500mg as needed. Schedule follow-up in one week to assess progress and adjust treatment plan.
